NAME

main.c - 🖥️ Server main entry point: multi-client connection manager with per-client rendering threads (60fps video + 172fps audio)

Thread-safe keyboard queue (lock-free SPSC ring buffer).

int server_main (void)
Server mode entry point for unified binary.

mixer_t *volatile g_audio_mixer = NULL
Global audio mixer instance for multi-client audio processing. h265_server_context_t *volatile g_h265_server = NULL
Global H.265 codec server context. static_cond_t g_shutdown_cond = STATIC_COND_INIT
Global shutdown condition variable for waking blocked threads. rate_limiter_t * g_rate_limiter = NULL
Global rate limiter for connection attempts and packet processing. bool g_server_encryption_enabled = false
Global flag indicating if server encryption is enabled. private_key_t g_server_private_key = {0}
Global server private key (first identity key, for backward compatibility). private_key_t g_server_identity_keys [MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS] = {0}
Global server identity keys array (multi-key support). size_t g_num_server_identity_keys = 0
Number of loaded server identity keys. public_key_t g_client_whitelist [MAX_CLIENTS] = {0}
Global client public key whitelist. size_t g_num_whitelisted_clients = 0
Number of whitelisted clients.

Detailed Description

🖥️ Server main entry point: multi-client connection manager with per-client rendering threads (60fps video + 172fps audio)

MODULAR COMPONENTS:

  • main.c (this file): Server initialization, signal handling, connection management
  • client.c: Per-client lifecycle, threading, and state management
  • protocol.c: Network packet processing and protocol implementation
  • stream.c: Video mixing, ASCII frame generation, and caching
  • render.c: Per-client rendering threads with rate limiting
  • stats.c: Performance monitoring and resource tracking

CONCURRENCY MODEL:

The server creates multiple thread types per client:

1.
Receive thread: Handles incoming packets from client (protocol.c functions)
2.
Send thread: Manages outgoing packet delivery (client.c)
3.
Video render thread: Generates ASCII frames at 60fps (render.c)
4.
Audio render thread: Mixes audio streams at 172fps (render.c)
5.
Stats logger thread: Periodic performance reporting (stats.c)

Thread Safety:

  • Lock ordering: Always acquire g_client_manager_rwlock BEFORE per-client mutexes
  • Snapshot pattern: Copy client state under mutex, then process without locks
  • Signal-safe shutdown: SIGINT handler only sets flags and closes sockets
  • Deterministic cleanup: Main thread waits for all worker threads before exit

WHY THE REFACTORING:

The original server.c became unmaintainable at 2408+ lines, making it:

  • Too large for LLM context windows (limited AI-assisted development)
  • Difficult for humans to navigate and understand
  • Slow to compile and modify
  • Hard to isolate bugs and add new features

The modular design enables:

  • Faster development cycles (smaller compilation units)
  • Better IDE support (jump-to-definition, IntelliSense)
  • Easier testing and debugging (isolated components)
  • Future extensibility (new protocols, renderers, optimizations)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Version

2.0 (Post-Modularization)

Definition in file server/main.c.

Function Documentation

int server_main (void )

Server mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete server lifecycle including:

  • Server-specific initialization (crypto, shutdown callback)
  • Network socket setup and binding
  • Main connection accept loop
  • Client lifecycle management
  • Graceful shutdown and cleanup

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher before this function is called, so they are available via global opt_* variables.

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Example

# Invoked by dispatcher after options are parsed:
ascii-chat server --port 8080
# Options parsed in main.c, then server_main() called

Definition at line 1774 of file server/main.c.

1774                       {
1775   // Common initialization (options, logging, lock debugging) now happens in main.c before dispatch
1776   // This function focuses on server-specific initialization
1777 
1778   // Register atomic for debug tracking
1779   ATOMIC_REGISTER_AUTO(g_should_exit, NULL);
1780 
1781   // Register shutdown check callback for library code
1782   shutdown_register_callback(check_shutdown);
1783 
1784   // Initialize status screen log buffer if:
1785   // 1. Terminal is interactive AND status_screen is enabled, OR

1787   // In non-interactive mode without explicit flag, logs go directly to stdout/stderr
1788   if ((GET_OPTION(status_screen) && terminal_is_interactive()) ||
1789       (GET_OPTION(status_screen_explicitly_set) && GET_OPTION(status_screen))) {
1790     ui_status_log_init();
1791 
1792     // Initialize interactive grep for status screen
1793     log_search_init();

1800     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Crypto initialization failed");
1801     FATAL(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto initialization failed");
1802   }
1803   log_debug("Crypto initialized successfully");
1804 
1805   // Handle keepawake: check for mutual exclusivity and apply mode default
1806   // Server default: keepawake DISABLED (use --keepawake to enable)
1807   if (GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake) && GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake)) {
1808     FATAL(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "--keepawake and --no-keepawake are mutually exclusive");
1809   }
1810   if (GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake)) {
1811     (void)platform_enable_keepawake();

1814   log_info("ascii-chat server starting...");
1815 
1816   // log_info("SERVER: Options initialized, using log file: %s", log_filename);
1817   int port = GET_OPTION(port);

1820     FATAL(ERROR_CONFIG, "Invalid port configuration: %d", port);
1821   }
1822 
1823   ascii_simd_init();
1824   precalc_rgb_palettes(weight_red, weight_green, weight_blue);
1825 
1826   // Simple signal handling (temporarily disable complex threading signal handling)
1827   log_debug("Setting up simple signal handlers...");
1828 
1829   // Handle Ctrl+C for cleanup
1830   platform_signal(SIGINT, server_handle_sigint);
1831   // Handle termination signal (SIGTERM is defined with limited support on Windows)
1832   platform_signal(SIGTERM, server_handle_sigterm);
1833 #ifndef _WIN32
1834   // SIGPIPE not supported on Windows
1835   platform_signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
1836   // Note: SIGUSR1 is registered in src/main.c for all modes
1837 #endif
1838 
1839 #ifndef NDEBUG
1840   // Lock debug thread is now started in src/main.c (all modes)
1841   // Initialize statistics system
1842   if (stats_init() != 0) {
1843     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Statistics system initialization failed");
1844   }
1845 #endif
1846 
1847   // Create background worker thread pool for server operations
1848   g_server_worker_pool = thread_pool_create("server_workers");
1849   if (!g_server_worker_pool) {
1850     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create server worker thread pool");
1851     FATAL(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create server worker thread pool");
1852   }
1853 
1854   // Spawn statistics logging thread in worker pool
1855   if (thread_pool_spawn(g_server_worker_pool, stats_logger_thread, NULL, 0, "stats_logger") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1856     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Statistics logger thread creation failed");
1857   } else {
1858     log_debug("Statistics logger thread started");
1859   }
1860 
1861   // Network setup - Use tcp_server abstraction for dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 binding
1862   log_debug("Config check: GET_OPTION(address)='%s', GET_OPTION(address6)='%s'", GET_OPTION(address),
1863             GET_OPTION(address6));
1864 
1865   bool ipv4_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address)) > 0);
1866   bool ipv6_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address6)) > 0);
1867   // Check if address is localhost (any loopback address, not just exact default)
1868   bool ipv4_is_default = is_localhost_ipv4(GET_OPTION(address));
1869   bool ipv6_is_default = is_localhost_ipv6(GET_OPTION(address6));
1870 
1871   log_debug("Binding decision: ipv4_has_value=%d, ipv6_has_value=%d, ipv4_is_default=%d, ipv6_is_default=%d",
1872             ipv4_has_value, ipv6_has_value, ipv4_is_default, ipv6_is_default);
1873 
1874   // Determine bind configuration
1875   bool bind_ipv4 = false;
1876   bool bind_ipv6 = false;
1877   const char *ipv4_address = NULL;
1878   const char *ipv6_address = NULL;
1879 
1880   if (ipv4_has_value && ipv6_has_value && ipv4_is_default && ipv6_is_default) {
1881     // Both are defaults: dual-stack with default localhost addresses
1882     bind_ipv4 = true;
1883     bind_ipv6 = true;
1884     ipv4_address = "127.0.0.1";
1885     ipv6_address = "::1";
1886     log_info("Default dual-stack: binding to 127.0.0.1 (IPv4) and ::1 (IPv6)");
1887   } else if (ipv4_has_value && !ipv4_is_default && (!ipv6_has_value || ipv6_is_default)) {
1888     // IPv4 explicitly set, IPv6 is default or empty: bind only IPv4
1889     bind_ipv4 = true;
1890     bind_ipv6 = false;
1891     ipv4_address = GET_OPTION(address);
1892     log_info("Binding only to IPv4 address: %s", ipv4_address);
1893   } else if (ipv6_has_value && !ipv6_is_default && (ipv4_is_default || !ipv4_has_value)) {
1894     // IPv6 explicitly set, IPv4 is default or empty: bind only IPv6
1895     bind_ipv4 = false;
1896     bind_ipv6 = true;
1897     ipv6_address = GET_OPTION(address6);
1898     log_info("Binding only to IPv6 address: %s", ipv6_address);
1899   } else {
1900     // Both explicitly set or one explicit + one default: dual-stack
1901     bind_ipv4 = true;
1902     bind_ipv6 = true;
1903     ipv4_address = ipv4_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address) : "127.0.0.1";
1904     ipv6_address = ipv6_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address6) : "::1";
1905     log_info("Dual-stack binding: IPv4=%s, IPv6=%s", ipv4_address, ipv6_address);
1906   }
1907 
1908   // Create server context - encapsulates all server state for passing to client handlers
1909   // This reduces global state and improves modularity by using tcp_server.user_data
1910   // Create H.265 server decoder context for multi-client codec support
1911   h265_server_context_t *h265_server_ctx = h265_server_context_create();
1912   if (!h265_server_ctx) {
1913     log_error("Failed to create H.265 server context");
1914     FATAL(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Cannot initialize H.265 codec support");

1916 
1917   server_context_t server_ctx = {
1918       .tcp_server = &g_tcp_server,

1923       .audio_mixer = g_audio_mixer,
1924       .stats = &g_stats,
1925       .stats_mutex = &g_stats_mutex,

1934   // Configure TCP server
1935   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
1936       .port = port,
1937       .ipv4_address = ipv4_address,
1938       .ipv6_address = ipv6_address,
1939       .bind_ipv4 = bind_ipv4,
1940       .bind_ipv6 = bind_ipv6,
1941       .accept_timeout_sec = ACCEPT_TIMEOUT,
1942       .client_handler = ascii_chat_client_handler,

1947 
1948   // Initialize TCP server (creates and binds sockets)
1949   memset(&g_tcp_server, 0, sizeof(g_tcp_server));
1950   asciichat_error_t tcp_init_result = tcp_server_init(&g_tcp_server, &tcp_config);
1951   if (tcp_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1952     // Signal shutdown to allow threads to exit before we call FATAL
1953     atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1954     log_debug("TCP server init failed, signaling shutdown to threads");
1955 
1956     // Clean up worker thread pool if it was created
1957     if (g_server_worker_pool) {
1958       log_debug("Early cleanup: destroying server worker pool");
1959       thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
1960       g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
1961     }
1962 
1963     FATAL(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize TCP server");
1964   }
1965

1967   websocket_server_config_t ws_config = {
1968       .port = GET_OPTION(websocket_port),

1974 
1975   // Log WSS configuration if provided

1979   }
1980 
1981   memset(&g_websocket_server, 0, sizeof(g_websocket_server));
1982   asciichat_error_t ws_init_result = websocket_server_init(&g_websocket_server, &ws_config);

1984     log_warn("Failed to initialize WebSocket server - browser clients will not be supported");
1985   } else {
1986     log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d", GET_OPTION(websocket_port));
1987   }
1988 
1989   // DEBUG: Print lock state immediately after WebSocket init
1990   log_info("★★★ LOCK STATE AFTER WEBSOCKET INIT ★★★");
1991   // debug_sync_print_state();  // Disabled: causes AddressSanitizer stack-use-after-return crash
1992 
1993   // =========================================================================
1994   // UPnP Port Mapping (Quick Win for Direct TCP)
1995   // =========================================================================
1996   // Track UPnP success for ACDS session type decision
1997   // If UPnP fails, we need to create a WebRTC session to enable client connectivity
1998   bool upnp_succeeded = false;
1999 
2000   // Try to open port via UPnP so direct TCP works for ~70% of home users.
2001   // If this fails, clients fall back to WebRTC automatically - not fatal.
2002   //
2003   // Strategy:
2004   //   1. UPnP (works on ~90% of home routers)
2005   //   2. NAT-PMP fallback (Apple routers)
2006   //   3. If both fail: use ACDS + WebRTC (reliable, but slightly higher latency)
2007   if (GET_OPTION(enable_upnp)) {
2008     asciichat_error_t upnp_result = nat_upnp_open(port, "ascii-chat Server", &g_upnp_ctx);
2009 
2010     if (upnp_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && g_upnp_ctx) {
2011       char public_addr[22];
2012       if (nat_upnp_get_address(g_upnp_ctx, public_addr, sizeof(public_addr)) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2013         char msg[256];
2014         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "🌐 Public endpoint: %s (direct TCP)", public_addr);
2015         log_console(LOG_INFO, msg);
2016         log_info("UPnP: Port mapping successful, public endpoint: %s", public_addr);
2017         upnp_succeeded = true;
2018       }
2019     } else {
2020       log_info("UPnP: Port mapping unavailable or failed - will use WebRTC fallback");
2021       log_console(LOG_INFO, "📡 Clients behind strict NATs will use WebRTC fallback");
2022     }
2023   } else {
2024     log_debug("UPnP: Disabled (use --upnp to enable automatic port mapping)");
2025   }
2026 
2027   // Initialize synchronization primitives
2028   if (rwlock_init(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "clients") != 0) {
2029     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize client manager rwlock");
2030   }
2031 
2032   // Register global sync primitives for debugging
2033   NAMED_REGISTER_RWLOCK(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "client_manager_rwlock", NULL);
2034 
2035   // Lock debug system already initialized earlier in main()
2036 
2037   // Check if SIGINT/SIGTERM was received during initialization
2038   // If so, skip the accept loop entirely and go to cleanup
2039   if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2040     log_info("Shutdown signal received during initialization, skipping server startup");
2041     goto cleanup;
2042   }
2043 
2044   // Lock debug thread already started earlier in main()
2045 
2046   // NOTE: g_client_manager is already zero-initialized in client.c with = {0}
2047   // We only need to initialize the mutex
2048   mutex_init(&g_client_manager.mutex, "client_manager");
2049 
2050   // Initialize uthash head pointer for O(1) lookup (uthash requires NULL initialization)
2051   g_client_manager.clients_by_id = NULL;
2052 
2053   // Initialize connection rate limiter (prevents DoS attacks)
2054   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2055     log_debug("Initializing connection rate limiter...");
2056     g_rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_memory();
2057     if (!g_rate_limiter) {
2058       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize rate limiter");
2059       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2060         FATAL(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create connection rate limiter");
2061       }
2062     } else {
2063       log_info("Connection rate limiter initialized (50 connections/min per IP)");
2064     }
2065   }
2066 
2067   // Initialize audio mixer (always enabled on server)
2068   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2069     log_debug("Initializing audio mixer for per-client audio rendering...");
2070     g_audio_mixer = mixer_create(MAX_CLIENTS, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
2071     if (!g_audio_mixer) {
2072       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize audio mixer");
2073       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2074         FATAL(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to initialize audio mixer");
2075       }
2076     } else {
2077       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2078         log_debug("Audio mixer initialized successfully for per-client audio rendering");
2079       }
2080     }
2081   }
2082 
2083   // Initialize H.265 codec server context (always enabled on server)
2084   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2085     log_debug("Initializing H.265 codec server context...");
2086     g_h265_server = h265_server_context_create();
2087     if (!g_h265_server) {
2088       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize H.265 codec context");
2089       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2090         FATAL(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to initialize H.265 codec context");
2091       }
2092     } else {
2093       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2094         log_debug("H.265 codec server context initialized successfully");
2095       }
2096     }
2097   }
2098 
2099   // Initialize mDNS context for LAN service discovery (optional)
2100   // mDNS allows clients on the LAN to discover this server without knowing its IP
2101   // Can be disabled with --no-mdns-advertise
2102   // Note: Actual advertisement is deferred until after ACDS session creation (if --acds is enabled)
2103   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2104     log_debug("Initializing mDNS for LAN service discovery...");
2105     g_mdns_ctx = asciichat_mdns_init();
2106     if (!g_mdns_ctx) {
2107       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize mDNS (non-fatal, LAN discovery disabled)");
2108       log_warn("mDNS disabled - LAN service discovery will not be available");
2109       g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2110     } else {
2111       log_debug("mDNS context initialized, advertisement deferred until session string is ready");
2112     }
2113   } else if (GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2114     log_info("mDNS service advertisement disabled via --no-mdns-advertise");
2115   }
2116 
2117   // ========================================================================
2118   // Session Host Creation (for discovery mode support)
2119   // ========================================================================
2120   // Create session_host to track clients in a transport-agnostic way.
2121   // This enables future discovery mode where participants can become hosts.
2122   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2123     session_host_config_t host_config = {
2124         .port = port,
2125         .ipv4_address = ipv4_address,
2126         .ipv6_address = ipv6_address,
2127         .max_clients = GET_OPTION(max_clients),
2128         .encryption_enabled = g_server_encryption_enabled,
2129         .key_path = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key),
2130         .password = GET_OPTION(password),
2131         .callbacks = {0}, // No callbacks for now
2132         .user_data = NULL,
2133     };
2134 
2135     server_ctx.session_host = session_host_create(&host_config);
2136     if (!server_ctx.session_host) {
2137       // Non-fatal: session_host is optional, server can work without it
2138       log_warn("Failed to create session_host (discovery mode support disabled)");
2139     } else {
2140       log_debug("Session host created for discovery mode support");
2141     }
2142   }
2143 
2144   // ========================================================================
2145   // MAIN CONNECTION LOOP - Delegated to tcp_server
2146   // ========================================================================
2147   //
2148   // The tcp_server module handles:
2149   // 1. Dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 accept loop with select() timeout
2150   // 2. Spawning client_handler threads for each connection
2151   // 3. Responsive shutdown when g_tcp_server.running is set to false
2152   //
2153   // Client lifecycle is managed by ascii_chat_client_handler() which:
2154   // - Performs rate limiting
2155   // - Calls add_client() to initialize structures and spawn workers
2156   // - Blocks until client disconnects
2157   // - Calls remove_client() to cleanup and stop worker threads
2158 
2159   // ACDS Session Creation: Register this server with discovery service
2160   // This also determines the session string for mDNS (if --acds is enabled)
2161   char session_string[64] = {0};
2162   bool session_is_mdns_only = false;
2163 
2164   // ACDS Registration (conditional on --discovery flag)
2165   if (GET_OPTION(discovery)) {
2166     // Security Requirement Check (Issue #239):
2167     // Server IP must be protected by password, identity verification, or explicit opt-in
2168 
2169     // Auto-detection: Check if password or identity verification is configured
2170     const char *password = GET_OPTION(password);
2171     bool has_password = password && strlen(password) > 0;
2172     const char *encrypt_key = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key);
2173     bool has_identity = encrypt_key && strlen(encrypt_key) > 0;
2174     bool explicit_expose = GET_OPTION(discovery_expose_ip) != 0;
2175 
2176     // Validate security configuration BEFORE attempting ACDS connection
2177     bool acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2178 
2179     if (has_password || has_identity) {
2180       // Auto-enable privacy: IP revealed only after verification
2181       acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2182       log_plain("🔒 ACDS privacy enabled: IP disclosed only after %s verification",
2183                 has_password ? "password" : "identity");
2184     } else if (explicit_expose) {
2185       // Explicit opt-in to public IP disclosure
2186       // Only prompt if running interactively (can prompt user)
2187       // When non-interactive (automated/scripted), treat explicit flag as confirmation
2188       bool is_interactive = terminal_can_prompt_user();
2189 
2190       if (is_interactive) {
2191         log_plain_stderr("");
2192         log_plain_stderr("⚠️  WARNING: You are about to allow PUBLIC IP disclosure!");
2193         log_plain_stderr("⚠️  Anyone with the session string will be able to see your IP address.");
2194         log_plain_stderr("⚠️  This is NOT RECOMMENDED unless you understand the privacy implications.");
2195         log_plain_stderr("");
2196 
2197         if (!platform_prompt_yes_no("Do you want to proceed with public IP disclosure", false)) {
2198           log_plain_stderr("");
2199           log_plain_stderr("❌ IP disclosure not confirmed. Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2200           goto skip_acds_session;
2201         }
2202       }
2203 
2204       // User confirmed (or running non-interactively with explicit flag) - proceed with public IP disclosure
2205       acds_expose_ip_flag = true;
2206       log_plain_stderr("");
2207       log_plain_stderr("⚠️  Public IP disclosure CONFIRMED");
2208       log_plain_stderr("⚠️  Your IP address will be visible to anyone with the session string");
2209     } else {
2210       // Security violation: No password, no identity, no explicit opt-in
2211       log_plain_stderr("❌ Cannot create ACDS session: No security configured!");
2212       log_plain_stderr("   You must either:");
2213       log_plain_stderr("   1. Set a password: --password \"your-secret\"");
2214       log_plain_stderr("   2. Use identity key: --key ~/.ssh/id_ed25519");
2215       log_plain_stderr("   3. Explicitly allow public IP: --acds-expose-ip (NOT RECOMMENDED)");
2216       log_plain_stderr("");
2217       log_plain_stderr("Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2218       goto skip_acds_session;
2219     }
2220 
2221     // Security is configured, proceed with ACDS connection
2222     const char *acds_server = GET_OPTION(discovery_server);
2223     uint16_t acds_port = (uint16_t)GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
2224 
2225     log_info("Attempting to create session on ACDS server at %s:%d...", acds_server, acds_port);
2226 
2227     acds_client_config_t acds_config;
2228     acds_client_config_init_defaults(&acds_config);
2229     SAFE_STRNCPY(acds_config.server_address, acds_server, sizeof(acds_config.server_address));
2230     acds_config.server_port = acds_port;
2231     acds_config.timeout_ms = 5 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
2232 
2233     // Allocate ACDS client on heap for server lifecycle
2234     g_acds_client = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_t), acds_client_t *);
2235     if (!g_acds_client) {
2236       log_error("Failed to allocate ACDS client");
2237       goto skip_acds_session;
2238     }
2239 
2240     asciichat_error_t acds_connect_result = acds_client_connect(g_acds_client, &acds_config);
2241     if (acds_connect_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2242       log_error("Failed to connect to ACDS server at %s:%d: %s", acds_server, acds_port,
2243                 asciichat_error_string(acds_connect_result));
2244       goto skip_acds_session;
2245     }
2246     if (acds_connect_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2247       // Prepare session creation parameters
2248       acds_session_create_params_t create_params;
2249       memset(&create_params, 0, sizeof(create_params));
2250 
2251       // Use server's Ed25519 identity public key if available
2252       if (g_server_encryption_enabled && has_identity) {
2253         memcpy(create_params.identity_pubkey, g_server_private_key.public_key, 32);
2254         log_debug("Using server identity key for ACDS session");
2255       } else {
2256         // No identity key available - use zero key
2257         // ACDS will accept this if identity verification is not required
2258         memset(create_params.identity_pubkey, 0, 32);
2259         log_debug("No server identity key - using zero key for ACDS session");
2260       }
2261 
2262       create_params.capabilities = 0x03; // Video + Audio
2263       create_params.max_participants = GET_OPTION(max_clients);
2264       log_debug("ACDS: max_clients option value = %d", GET_OPTION(max_clients));
2265 
2266       // Set password if configured
2267       create_params.has_password = has_password;
2268       if (has_password) {
2269         // TODO: Hash password with Argon2id
2270         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.password, password, sizeof(create_params.password));
2271       }
2272 
2273       // Set IP disclosure policy (determined above)
2274       create_params.acds_expose_ip = acds_expose_ip_flag;
2275       log_info("DEBUG: Server setting acds_expose_ip=%d (explicit_expose=%d, has_password=%d, has_identity=%d)",
2276                create_params.acds_expose_ip, explicit_expose, has_password, has_identity);
2277 
2278       // Set session type (Direct TCP or WebRTC)
2279       // Auto-detect: Use WebRTC if UPnP failed OR if explicitly requested via --webrtc
2280       // Exception: If bind address is 0.0.0.0, server is on public IP - use Direct TCP
2281       const char *bind_addr = GET_OPTION(address);
2282       bool bind_all_interfaces = (strcmp(bind_addr, "0.0.0.0") == 0);
2283 
2284       // Determine session type: prefer WebRTC by default (unless explicitly disabled)
2285       // Priority: explicit --webrtc flag > connection type detection > UPnP > default
2286       if (GET_OPTION(webrtc)) {
2287         // Explicit WebRTC request
2288         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2289         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (explicitly requested via --webrtc)");
2290       } else if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2291         // Bind to 0.0.0.0: use WebRTC as default (better NAT compatibility)
2292         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2293         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (default for 0.0.0.0 binding, provides NAT-agnostic connections)");
2294       } else if (upnp_succeeded) {
2295         // UPnP port mapping worked - can use direct TCP
2296         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_DIRECT_TCP;
2297         log_info("ACDS session type: Direct TCP (UPnP succeeded, server is publicly accessible)");
2298       } else {
2299         // UPnP failed and not on public IP - use WebRTC for NAT traversal
2300         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2301         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (UPnP failed, server behind NAT)");
2302       }
2303 
2304       // Server connection information (where clients should connect)
2305       // If bind address is 0.0.0.0, leave server_address empty for ACDS to auto-detect public IP
2306       if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2307         create_params.server_address[0] = '\0'; // Empty - ACDS will use connection source IP
2308         log_debug("Bind address is 0.0.0.0, ACDS will auto-detect public IP from connection");
2309       } else {
2310         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.server_address, bind_addr, sizeof(create_params.server_address));
2311       }
2312       create_params.server_port = port;
2313 
2314       // DEBUG: Log what we're sending to ACDS
2315       log_info("DEBUG: Before SESSION_CREATE - expose_ip_publicly=%d, server_address='%s' port=%u, session_type=%u",
2316                create_params.acds_expose_ip, create_params.server_address, create_params.server_port,
2317                create_params.session_type);
2318 
2319       // Create session
2320       acds_session_create_result_t create_result;
2321       asciichat_error_t create_err = acds_session_create(g_acds_client, &create_params, &create_result);
2322 
2323       if (create_err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2324         SAFE_STRNCPY(session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(session_string));
2325         SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2326         session_is_mdns_only = false; // Session is now registered with ACDS (globally discoverable)
2327         log_info("Session created: %s", session_string);
2328 
2329         // Server must join its own session so ACDS can route signaling messages
2330         log_debug("Server joining session as first participant for WebRTC signaling...");
2331         acds_session_join_params_t join_params = {0};
2332         join_params.session_string = session_string;
2333 
2334         // Use same identity key as session creation
2335         memcpy(join_params.identity_pubkey, create_params.identity_pubkey, 32);
2336 
2337         // Include password if session is password-protected
2338         if (has_password) {
2339           join_params.has_password = true;
2340           SAFE_STRNCPY(join_params.password, password, sizeof(join_params.password));
2341         }
2342 
2343         acds_session_join_result_t join_result = {0};
2344         asciichat_error_t join_err = acds_session_join(g_acds_client, &join_params, &join_result);
2345         if (join_err != ASCIICHAT_OK || !join_result.success) {
2346           log_error("Failed to join own session: %s (error: %s)", asciichat_error_string(join_err),
2347                     join_result.error_message[0] ? join_result.error_message : "unknown");
2348           // Continue anyway - this is not fatal for Direct TCP sessions
2349         } else {
2350           log_debug("Server joined session successfully (participant_id: %02x%02x...)", join_result.participant_id[0],
2351                     join_result.participant_id[1]);
2352           // Store participant ID for WebRTC signaling (needed to identify server in SDP/ICE messages)
2353           memcpy(g_server_participant_id, join_result.participant_id, 16);
2354           log_debug("Stored server participant_id for signaling: %02x%02x...", g_server_participant_id[0],
2355                     g_server_participant_id[1]);
2356           memcpy(create_result.session_id, join_result.session_id, 16);
2357         }
2358 
2359         // Keep ACDS connection alive for WebRTC signaling relay
2360         log_debug("Server staying connected to ACDS for signaling relay");
2361 
2362         // Create ACDS transport wrapper for sending signaling packets
2363         g_acds_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("transport_acds_server", g_acds_client->socket, NULL);
2364         if (!g_acds_transport) {
2365           log_error("Failed to create ACDS transport wrapper");
2366         } else {
2367           log_debug("ACDS transport wrapper created for signaling");
2368 
2369           // Start ACDS ping thread to keep connection alive (for ALL session types)
2370           int ping_thread_result = asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_ping_thread, "acds_ping", acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2371           if (ping_thread_result != 0) {
2372             log_error("Failed to create ACDS ping thread: %d", ping_thread_result);
2373           } else {
2374             log_debug("ACDS ping thread started to keep connection alive");
2375             g_acds_ping_thread_started = true;
2376           }
2377         }
2378 
2379         // Initialize WebRTC peer_manager if session type is WebRTC
2380         if (create_params.session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2381           log_debug("Initializing WebRTC library and peer manager for session (role=CREATOR)...");
2382 
2383           // Initialize WebRTC library (libdatachannel)
2384           asciichat_error_t webrtc_init_result = webrtc_init();
2385           if (webrtc_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2386             log_error("Failed to initialize WebRTC library: %s", asciichat_error_string(webrtc_init_result));
2387             g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2388           } else {
2389             log_debug("WebRTC library initialized successfully");
2390 
2391             // Configure STUN servers for ICE gathering (static to persist for peer_manager lifetime)
2392             static stun_server_t stun_servers[4] = {0};
2393             static lifecycle_t g_stun_lc = LIFECYCLE_INIT;
2394             static int stun_count = 0; // Store actual count separately
2395 
2396             if (lifecycle_init(&g_stun_lc, "stun")) {
2397               log_debug("Parsing STUN servers from options: '%s'", GET_OPTION(stun_servers));
2398               int count =
2399                   stun_servers_parse(GET_OPTION(stun_servers), OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, stun_servers, 4);
2400               if (count > 0) {
2401                 stun_count = count;
2402                 log_debug("Parsed %d STUN servers", count);
2403                 for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
2404                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2405                 }
2406               } else {
2407                 log_warn("Failed to parse STUN servers, using defaults");
2408                 stun_count = stun_servers_parse(OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT,
2409                                                 stun_servers, 4);
2410                 log_debug("Using default STUN servers, count=%d", stun_count);
2411                 for (int i = 0; i < stun_count; i++) {
2412                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2413                 }
2414               }
2415             }
2416 
2417             // Configure peer_manager
2418             webrtc_peer_manager_config_t pm_config = {
2419                 .role = WEBRTC_ROLE_CREATOR, // Server accepts offers, generates answers
2420                 .stun_servers = stun_servers,
2421                 .stun_count = stun_count,
2422                 .turn_servers = NULL, // No TURN for server (clients should have public IP or use TURN)
2423                 .turn_count = 0,
2424                 .on_transport_ready = on_webrtc_transport_ready,
2425                 .user_data = &server_ctx,
2426                 .crypto_ctx = NULL // WebRTC handles crypto internally
2427             };
2428 
2429             // Configure signaling callbacks for relaying SDP/ICE via ACDS
2430             webrtc_signaling_callbacks_t signaling_callbacks = {
2431                 .send_sdp = server_send_sdp, .send_ice = server_send_ice, .user_data = NULL};
2432 
2433             // Create peer_manager
2434             asciichat_error_t pm_result =
2435                 webrtc_peer_manager_create(&pm_config, &signaling_callbacks, &g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2436             if (pm_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2437               log_error("Failed to create WebRTC peer_manager: %s", asciichat_error_string(pm_result));
2438               g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2439             } else {
2440               log_debug("WebRTC peer_manager initialized successfully");
2441 
2442               // Start ACDS receive thread for WebRTC signaling relay
2443               int thread_result =
2444                   asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_receive_thread, "acds_recv", acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2445               if (thread_result != 0) {
2446                 log_error("Failed to create ACDS receive thread: %d", thread_result);
2447                 // Cleanup peer_manager since signaling won't work
2448                 webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2449                 g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2450               } else {
2451                 log_debug("ACDS receive thread started for WebRTC signaling relay");
2452                 g_acds_receive_thread_started = true;
2453               }
2454             }
2455           } // Close else block from webrtc_init() success
2456         } else {
2457           log_debug("Session type is DIRECT_TCP, skipping WebRTC peer_manager initialization");
2458         }
2459 
2460         // Advertise mDNS with ACDS session string
2461         // This ensures both mDNS and ACDS discovery return the same session string
2462         advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2463       } else {
2464         log_warn("Failed to create session on ACDS server (server will run without discovery)");
2465         // Clean up failed ACDS client
2466         if (g_acds_client) {
2467           acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2468           SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2469           g_acds_client = NULL;
2470         }
2471       }
2472     } else {
2473       log_warn("Could not connect to ACDS server at %s:%d (server will run without discovery)", acds_server, acds_port);
2474     }
2475   } else {
2476     log_info("ACDS registration disabled (use --acds to enable)");
2477   }
2478 
2479 skip_acds_session:
2480   // Fallback: If no session string was set by ACDS (either disabled or failed),
2481   // generate a random session string for mDNS discovery only
2482   if (session_string[0] == '\0' && g_mdns_ctx) {
2483     log_debug("No ACDS session string available, generating random session for mDNS");
2484 
2485     // Use the proper session string generation from discovery module
2486     // This generates adjective-noun-noun format using the full wordlists
2487     if (acds_string_generate(session_string, sizeof(session_string)) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2488       log_error("Failed to generate session string for mDNS");
2489       return 1;
2490     }
2491 
2492     log_debug("Generated random session string for mDNS: '%s'", session_string);
2493 
2494     // Mark that this session is mDNS-only (not globally discoverable via ACDS)
2495     session_is_mdns_only = true;
2496 
2497     // Advertise mDNS with random session string
2498     advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2499   }
2500 
2501   // ====================================================================
2502   // Display session string prominently as the FINAL startup message
2503   // This ensures users see the connection info clearly without logs
2504   // wiping it away
2505   // ====================================================================
2506   if (session_string[0] != '\0') {
2507     if (session_is_mdns_only) {
2508       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s (LAN only via "
2509                 "mDNS)\n🔗 Share with others on your LAN to join:\n   ascii-chat "
2510                 "%s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2511                 session_string, session_string);
2512     } else {
2513       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s\n🔗 Share this "
2514                 "globally to join:\n   ascii-chat %s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2515                 session_string, session_string);
2516     }
2517   }
2518 
2519   // Copy session info to globals for status screen display
2520   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2521   g_session_is_mdns_only = session_is_mdns_only;
2522 
2523   log_debug("Server entering accept loop (port %d)...", port);
2524 
2525   // Initialize status screen
2526   g_server_start_time = time(NULL);
2527   g_last_status_update = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();
2528 
2529   // Clear status screen log buffer to discard initialization logs
2530   // This ensures only NEW logs (generated after status screen starts) are displayed
2531   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2532     ui_status_log_clear();
2533   }
2534 
2535   // Start status screen thread if enabled
2536   // Runs independently at target FPS (default 60 Hz), decoupled from network accept loop
2537   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2538     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_status_screen_thread, "status_screen", status_screen_thread, NULL) != 0) {
2539       log_error("Failed to create status screen thread");
2540       goto cleanup;
2541     }
2542     log_debug("Status screen thread started");
2543   }
2544 
2545   // Start WebSocket server thread if initialized
2546   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2547     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_websocket_server_thread, "ws_server", websocket_server_thread_wrapper,
2548                                 &g_websocket_server) != 0) {
2549       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket server thread");
2550     } else {
2551       g_websocket_server_thread_started = true;
2552       log_info("WebSocket server thread started");
2553     }
2554   }
2555 
2556   // Run TCP server (blocks until shutdown signal received)
2557   // tcp_server_run() handles:
2558   // - select() on IPv4/IPv6 sockets with timeout
2559   // - accept() new connections
2560   // - Spawn ascii_chat_client_handler() thread for each connection
2561   // - Responsive shutdown when atomic_store_bool(&g_tcp_server.running, false)
2562   asciichat_error_t run_result = tcp_server_run(&g_tcp_server);
2563   if (run_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2564     log_error("TCP server exited with error");
2565   }
2566 
2567   log_debug("Server accept loop exited");
2568 
2569 cleanup:
2570   // Signal status screen thread to exit
2571   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
2572 
2573   // Wait for status screen thread to finish if it was started
2574   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2575     log_debug("Waiting for status screen thread to exit...");
2576     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_status_screen_thread, NULL, NS_PER_SEC_INT); // 1 second
2577     if (join_result != 0) {
2578       log_warn("Status screen thread did not exit cleanly (timeout)");
2579     } else {
2580       log_debug("Status screen thread exited");
2581     }
2582   }
2583 
2584   // Cleanup
2585   log_debug("Server shutting down...");
2586   memset(g_session_string, 0, sizeof(g_session_string)); // Clear session string for status screen
2587 
2588   // Wake up any threads that might be blocked on condition variables
2589   // (like packet queues) to ensure responsive shutdown
2590   // This must happen BEFORE client cleanup to wake up any blocked threads
2591   static_cond_broadcast(&g_shutdown_cond);
2592   // NOTE: Do NOT call cond_destroy() on statically-initialized condition variables
2593   // g_shutdown_cond uses STATIC_COND_INIT which doesn't allocate resources that need cleanup
2594   // Calling cond_destroy() on a static cond is undefined behavior on some platforms
2595 
2596   // Close all client sockets immediately to unblock receive threads.
2597   // The signal handler only closed the listening socket, but client receive threads
2598   // are still blocked in recv_with_timeout(). We need to close their sockets to unblock them.
2599   log_debug("Closing all client sockets to unblock receive threads...");
2600 
2601   // Use write lock since we're modifying client->socket
2602   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2603   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2604     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2605     if (client->client_id[0] != '\0' && client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2606       socket_close(client->socket);
2607       client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
2608     }
2609   }
2610   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2611 
2612   log_debug("Signaling all clients to stop (sockets closed, g_should_exit set)...");
2613 
2614   // Stop and destroy server worker thread pool (stats logger, etc.)
2615   if (g_server_worker_pool) {
2616     thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
2617     g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
2618     log_debug("Server worker thread pool stopped");
2619   }
2620 
2621   // Destroy rate limiter
2622   if (g_rate_limiter) {
2623     rate_limiter_destroy(g_rate_limiter);
2624     g_rate_limiter = NULL;
2625   }
2626 
2627   // Shutdown WebSocket server BEFORE removing clients.
2628   // The LWS event thread runs callbacks that access transport data (impl_data).
2629   // If we destroy transports via remove_client while LWS is still running,
2630   // callbacks access freed memory (heap-use-after-free).
2631   // lws_context_destroy fires LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED for all connections, which
2632   // closes transports and NULLs the per-session conn_data->transport pointer.
2633   // After this, remove_client can safely destroy the transport objects.
2634   bool websocket_exited_cleanly = true;  // Default to clean unless timeout detected
2635   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2636     log_debug("Shutting down WebSocket server before client cleanup");
2637     atomic_store_u64(&g_websocket_server.running, false);
2638     // Wake the LWS event loop immediately instead of waiting for the 50ms
2639     // lws_service timeout. This is safe because the event loop thread can't
2640     // destroy the context until after it exits its loop (which requires
2641     // seeing running=false, which we just set).
2642     websocket_server_cancel_service(&g_websocket_server);
2643 
2644     // Join with 500ms timeout. If the WebSocket thread is deadlocked in lws_service(),
2645     // it won't exit and we skip the graceful handler thread cleanup to avoid prolonged
2646     // shutdown hangs. Forceful cleanup will happen in websocket_server_destroy().
2647     if (g_websocket_server_thread_started) {
2648       int join_result =
2649           asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_websocket_server_thread, NULL, 500 * NS_PER_MS_INT); // 500ms in ns
2650       if (join_result != 0) {
2651         log_warn("WebSocket thread did not exit within 500ms, skipping graceful handler cleanup");
2652         websocket_exited_cleanly = false;
2653       }
2654       g_websocket_server_thread_started = false;
2655     }
2656 
2657     if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2658       log_debug("WebSocket thread confirmed exited, waiting for handler threads to complete...");
2659     }
2660   }
2661 
2662   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit BEFORE destroying WebSocket context
2663   // Handler threads may still be working with WebSocket connections, so we must
2664   // ensure they complete before destroying the context (which would abort their operations).
2665   // Skip this if WebSocket thread didn't exit cleanly (deadlock detected).
2666   if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2667     int retry_count = 0;
2668     while (retry_count < 50) { // Max 500ms wait
2669       rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2670       bool all_cleaned = true;
2671       for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2672         if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0') {
2673           all_cleaned = false;
2674           break;
2675         }
2676       }
2677       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2678 
2679       if (all_cleaned)
2680         break;
2681       platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms
2682       retry_count++;
2683     }
2684     log_debug("Client handler threads cleaned up, destroying WebSocket server");
2685   } else {
2686     log_warn("Skipping graceful handler thread cleanup due to WebSocket deadlock");
2687   }
2688 
2689   // ★ CRITICAL: Cleanup debug sync BEFORE destroying websocket_server
2690   // The debug_sync thread runs concurrently and accesses the named registry.
2691   // If websocket_server_destroy() frees thread pool memory first, the debug thread
2692   // will get a use-after-free error trying to check condition variables.
2693   // Moving this before websocket destroy prevents the race condition.
2694 #ifndef NDEBUG
2695   debug_sync_destroy();
2696 #endif
2697 
2698   // Now destroy WebSocket server after all handler threads have completed
2699   // This ensures no handler threads are still accessing the context.
2700   // (It's safe to call even if context was already destroyed forcefully above)
2701   websocket_server_destroy(&g_websocket_server);
2702   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
2703 
2704   // Cleanup status screen log capture (must be AFTER all client threads exit to avoid use-after-free)
2705   // Client threads may still be appending logs during the wait loop above, so we destroy the buffer only
2706   // after we've confirmed they've all exited.
2707   ui_status_log_destroy();
2708 
2709   // Clean up all connected clients
2710   log_debug("Cleaning up connected clients...");
2711   // FIXED: Simplified to collect client IDs first, then remove them without holding locks
2712   char clients_to_remove[MAX_CLIENTS][MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
2713   int client_count = 0;
2714 
2715   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2716   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2717     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2718 
2719     // Only attempt to clean up clients that were actually connected
2720     // (client_id is empty string for uninitialized clients)
2721     // FIXED: Only access mutex for initialized clients to avoid accessing uninitialized mutex
2722     if (client->client_id[0] == '\0') {
2723       continue; // Skip uninitialized clients
2724     }
2725 
2726     // Use snapshot pattern to avoid holding both locks simultaneously
2727     // This prevents deadlock by not acquiring client_state_mutex while holding rwlock
2728     SAFE_STRNCPY(clients_to_remove[client_count], client->client_id, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN - 1);
2729 
2730     // Clean up ANY client that was allocated, whether active or not
2731     // (disconnected clients may not be active but still have resources)
2732     client_count++;
2733   }
2734   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2735 
2736   // Remove all clients without holding any locks
2737   for (int i = 0; i < client_count; i++) {
2738     if (remove_client(&server_ctx, clients_to_remove[i]) != 0) {
2739       log_error("Failed to remove client %s during shutdown", clients_to_remove[i]);
2740     }
2741   }
2742 
2743   // Clean up hash table
2744   // Cannot use HASH_ITER when deleting - HASH_DELETE modifies the table and can set
2745   // the pointer to NULL, causing HASH_ITER to dereference a null pointer.
2746   // Instead, repeatedly delete the first item until the table is empty.
2747   while (g_client_manager.clients_by_id) {
2748     client_info_t *current_client = g_client_manager.clients_by_id;
2749     HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, current_client);
2750     // Note: We don't free current_client here because it's part of the clients[] array
2751   }
2752 
2753   // Clean up audio mixer
2754   if (g_audio_mixer) {
2755     // Set to NULL first before destroying.
2756     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_audio_mixer.
2757     // Setting it to NULL first prevents use-after-free race condition.
2758     // volatile ensures this write is visible to other threads immediately
2759     mixer_t *mixer_to_destroy = g_audio_mixer;
2760     g_audio_mixer = NULL;
2761     mixer_destroy(mixer_to_destroy);
2762   }
2763 
2764   // Clean up H.265 codec server context
2765   if (g_h265_server) {
2766     // Set to NULL first before destroying, similar to g_audio_mixer
2767     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_h265_server.
2768     h265_server_context_t *h265_to_destroy = g_h265_server;
2769     g_h265_server = NULL;
2770     h265_server_context_destroy(h265_to_destroy);
2771     log_debug("H.265 codec server context shut down");
2772   }
2773 
2774   // Clean up mDNS context
2775   if (g_mdns_ctx) {
2776     asciichat_mdns_destroy(g_mdns_ctx);
2777     g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2778     log_debug("mDNS context shut down");
2779   }
2780 
2781   // Clean up synchronization primitives
2782   rwlock_destroy(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2783   mutex_destroy(&g_client_manager.mutex);
2784 
2785 #ifndef NDEBUG
2786   // Clean up statistics system
2787   stats_cleanup();
2788 
2789   // Note: debug_sync_destroy() is now called earlier (before websocket_server_destroy)
2790   // to avoid use-after-free race condition with the debug monitoring thread
2791 #endif
2792 
2793   // Destroy session host (before TCP server shutdown)
2794   if (server_ctx.session_host) {
2795     log_debug("Destroying session host");
2796     session_host_destroy(server_ctx.session_host);
2797     server_ctx.session_host = NULL;
2798   }
2799 
2800   // Destroy H.265 server codec context
2801   if (server_ctx.h265_server) {
2802     log_debug("Destroying H.265 server context");
2803     h265_server_context_destroy(server_ctx.h265_server);
2804     server_ctx.h265_server = NULL;
2805   }
2806 
2807   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets and cleans up)
2808   tcp_server_destroy(&g_tcp_server);
2809 
2810   // WebSocket server already shut down before client cleanup (above).
2811 
2812   // Join ACDS threads (if started)
2813   // NOTE: Must be done BEFORE destroying transport to ensure clean shutdown
2814   if (g_acds_ping_thread_started) {
2815     log_debug("Joining ACDS ping thread");
2816     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2817     g_acds_ping_thread_started = false;
2818     log_debug("ACDS ping thread joined");
2819   }
2820 
2821   if (g_acds_receive_thread_started) {
2822     log_debug("Joining ACDS receive thread");
2823     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2824     g_acds_receive_thread_started = false;
2825     log_debug("ACDS receive thread joined");
2826   }
2827 
2828   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (if initialized for ACDS signaling relay)
2829   if (g_webrtc_peer_manager) {
2830     log_debug("Destroying WebRTC peer manager");
2831     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2832     g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2833   }
2834 
2835   // Clean up ACDS transport wrapper (if created)
2836   if (g_acds_transport) {
2837     log_debug("Destroying ACDS transport wrapper");
2838     acip_transport_destroy(g_acds_transport);
2839     g_acds_transport = NULL;
2840   }
2841 
2842   // Disconnect from ACDS server (if connected for WebRTC signaling relay)
2843   if (g_acds_client) {
2844     log_debug("Disconnecting from ACDS server");
2845     acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2846     SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2847     g_acds_client = NULL;
2848   }
2849   // Disable keepawake mode (re-allow OS to sleep)
2850   platform_disable_keepawake();
2851 
2852   // Clean up platform-specific resources (Windows: Winsock cleanup, timer restoration)
2853   // POSIX: minimal cleanup (symbol cache already handled above on Windows)
2854   socket_cleanup();
2855 
2856   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
2857 
2858   asciichat_error_stats_print();
2859 
2860   // Use exit() to allow atexit() handlers to run
2861   // Cleanup functions are idempotent (check if initialized first)
2862   return 0;
2863 }

Variable
  Documentation




mixer_t*
  volatile g_audio_mixer = NULL


Global audio mixer instance for multi-client audio processing.
    Global audio mixer.




The mixer combines audio streams from multiple clients, excluding
    each client's own audio from their outbound stream (preventing echo).
    Created once during server initialization and shared by all audio render
    threads.




THREAD SAFETY: The mixer itself is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently by multiple render.c audio threads without external
    synchronization. During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
    use-after-free.


Definition at line 173 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    client_audio_render_thread(), remove_client(), and
    server_main().






public_key_t
  g_client_whitelist[MAX_CLIENTS] = {0}


Global client public key whitelist. Whitelist of allowed client
    public keys.




Array of public keys for clients that are authorized to connect to
    the server. Used for client authentication when whitelist mode is enabled.
    Sized to hold up to MAX_CLIENTS entries.




Note


Only used when client authentication is enabled



Accessed from crypto.c for client authentication




Definition at line 523 of file server/main.c.


523 {0};






Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    server_main().






h265_server_context_t* volatile g_h265_server = NULL


Global H.265 codec server context. Shared H.265 decoding context
    used by all packet handlers to decode client H.265 frames. Each client gets
    its own decoder instance.




THREAD SAFETY: The h265_server context is thread-safe and can be
    used concurrently by multiple client handlers. Decoders are per-client.
    During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
  use-after-free.


Definition at line 185 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), and
    server_main().






size_t
  g_num_server_identity_keys = 0


Number of loaded server identity keys. Number of server identity
    keys in use.




Tracks how many identity keys were successfully loaded from --key
    flags. Zero means server is running in simple mode (no identity key).


Definition at line 510 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake().






size_t
  g_num_whitelisted_clients = 0


Number of whitelisted clients. Tracks the current number of
    entries in g_client_whitelist that are valid and active. Used to iterate the
    whitelist and check authorization.


Definition at line 533 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    server_main().






rate_limiter_t*
  g_rate_limiter = NULL


Global rate limiter for connection attempts and packet processing.
    Global connection rate limiter.




In-memory rate limiter to prevent connection flooding and DoS
    attacks. Tracks connection attempts and packet rates per IP address with
    configurable limits.




Default limits (from rate_limit.c):


    
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONNECTION: 50 connections per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_IMAGE_FRAME: 144 FPS (8640 frames/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_AUDIO: 172 FPS (10320 packets/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_PING: 2 Hz (120 pings/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CLIENT_JOIN: 10 joins per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONTROL: 100 control packets per 60 seconds
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: The rate limiter is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently from the main accept loop and packet handlers without external
    synchronization.




See also


main.h for extern declaration


Definition at line 215 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by process_decrypted_packet(), and
    server_main().






bool g_server_encryption_enabled = false


Global flag indicating if server encryption is enabled. Server
    encryption enabled flag.




Set to true when the server is configured to use encryption and
    has successfully loaded a private key. Controls whether the server performs
    cryptographic handshakes with clients.




Note


Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto
  operations


Definition at line 474 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    server_main().






private_key_t
  g_server_identity_keys[MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS] = {0}


Global server identity keys array (multi-key support). Array of
    server identity keys.




Stores all server identity keys loaded from multiple --key flags.
    Enables servers to present different keys (SSH, GPG) based on client
    expectations. Server selects the appropriate key during handshake based on
    what the client downloaded from ACDS.




Note


g_server_private_key points to g_server_identity_keys[0]
  for compatibility


Definition at line 500 of file server/main.c.


500 {0};






Referenced by server_crypto_handshake().






private_key_t
  g_server_private_key = {0}


Global server private key (first identity key, for backward
    compatibility). Server's private key.




Stores the server's primary private key loaded from the first
    --key flag. Used for cryptographic handshakes and packet
    encryption/decryption. Initialized during server startup from the configured
    key file path.




Note


This is an alias to g_server_identity_keys[0] for
  backward compatibility



Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto operations

487 {0};

    server_main().






static_cond_t
  g_shutdown_cond = STATIC_COND_INIT


Global shutdown condition variable for waking blocked threads.
    Used to wake up threads that might be blocked on condition variables (like
    packet queues) during shutdown. This ensures responsive shutdown even when
    threads are waiting on blocking operations.


Definition at line 194 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_main().
